---
title: "Spring phenology tier2 project"
subtitle: "Preseason length moving window figures (PEP725 data)"
author: "Constantin Zohner"
date: "Last run: `r format(Sys.Date(), '%d %B %Y')`"

format:
  html:
    toc: false
    theme: default
    highlight-style: haddock
    df-print: paged
    embed-resources: true

execute:
  #warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
  output: true

always_allow_html: true
editor: visual
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

<br>

### Model names

-   Tmean: Daily mean air temperature
-   Tmax: Daily maximum air temperature

<br>

# 1. Setup

<details>

<summary>Packages</summary>

```{r, message=F}
require(tidyverse)
require(data.table)
require(broom)
require(patchwork)
require(MASS)
require(car)
require(lme4)
```

</details>

<details>

<summary>Plot theme</summary>

```{r}
plotTheme1 = theme(legend.position   = "right",
                   legend.background = element_rect(fill=NA, linewidth=0.5, linetype="solid"),
                   legend.text       = element_text(color="black",face = "italic"),
                   panel.grid.major  = element_blank(),
                   panel.grid.minor  = element_blank(),
                   panel.background  = element_blank(),
                   panel.border      = element_rect(colour = "black", fill=NA),
                   axis.line         = element_line(color = "black"),
                   axis.text         = element_text(colour = "black"),
                   axis.text.x       = element_text(angle = 45, hjust=1),
                   strip.background  = element_rect(fill=NA),
                   plot.title        = element_text(face="bold"))

palette_values = c('#a6cee3','#1f78b4','#b2df8a','#33a02c','#fb9a99',
                   '#e31a1c','#fdbf6f','#ff7f00','#cab2d6','#6a3d9a')
```

</details>

<details>

<summary>Directories</summary>

```{r}
#-------------------------------------------------
# Define the base directory
#-------------------------------------------------

base.dir = "/Users/goalie/Desktop/PhD/Publication_material/40_Spring_phenology_tier2/PEP725_Analysis"


#-------------------------------------------------
# Build specific directories from the base
#-------------------------------------------------

# PEP moving window data
PEP.dir = file.path(base.dir, "Analysis_input/PEP_drivers_final_spring_MW/MW15/Merged_file")

# Output
output.dir = file.path(base.dir, "Analysis_output/ModelFigures/Moving_windows/Preseason_periods")
```

</details>

<details>

<summary>Data</summary>

```{r}
PEP.df <- fread(paste(PEP.dir, "pep_MW15_merged_final.csv", sep="/")) %>% 
  distinct(phenology, species, s_id, MWyear, .keep_all = T) # delete duplicates
```

<br>

# 2. Analysis and Figures

<details>

<summary>Mixed effects models</summary>

```{r}
# Define variables and phenology groups
variables <- c("Tmean", "Tmax")
phenologies <- unique(PEP.df$phenology)

# Loop over each phenology group
for (phen in phenologies) {

  cat("\n=============================================\n")
  cat(paste0("Phenology group: ", phen, "\n"))
  cat("=============================================\n")
  
  # Subset data for current phenology
  phen_df <- PEP.df[PEP.df$phenology == phen, ]
  
  # Change in end date (leaf_out_mean assumed to exist for all phenologies)
  end_model <- lmer(leaf_out_mean ~ MWyear + (1 | timeseries) + (1 | species),
                    data = phen_df,
                    control = lmerControl(optimizer = "Nelder_Mead"))
  
  end_slope <- summary(end_model)$coefficients["MWyear", "Estimate"]
  end_SE <- summary(end_model)$coefficients["MWyear", "Std. Error"]*2
  cat(paste0("Change in end date = ", round(end_slope, 2), " ± ", round(end_SE, 4), " days per year\n"))
  
  # Loop through temperature variables
  for (variable in variables) {
    
    # Length change
    length_formula <- as.formula(paste0(variable, "_length ~ MWyear + (1 | timeseries) + (1 | species)"))
    length_model <- lmer(length_formula, data = phen_df, control = lmerControl(optimizer = "Nelder_Mead"))
    length_slope <- summary(length_model)$coefficients["MWyear", "Estimate"]
    length_SE    <- summary(length_model)$coefficients["MWyear", "Std. Error"]*2
    cat(paste0(variable, " change in length = ", round(length_slope, 2), " ± ", round(length_SE, 4), " days per year\n"))
    
    # Start date change
    start_formula <- as.formula(paste0(variable, "_start ~ MWyear + (1 | timeseries) + (1 | species)"))
    start_model <- lmer(start_formula, data = phen_df, control = lmerControl(optimizer = "Nelder_Mead"))
    start_slope <- summary(start_model)$coefficients["MWyear", "Estimate"]
    start_SE    <- summary(start_model)$coefficients["MWyear", "Std. Error"]*2
    cat(paste0(variable, " change in start date = ", round(start_slope, 2), " ± ", round(start_SE, 4), " days per year\n"))
  }
}
```

</details>

<details>

<summary>Final plots</summary>

```{r}
#==========================================
# Leaf-out observations
#==========================================

# start for loop
for (variable in variables) {
  
  #------------------------------------
  # 1. Extract data
  #------------------------------------
  
  PEP_obs.df = PEP.df %>% 
    # keep only leaf-out observations
    filter(phenology == "leaf_out")
    
  #------------------------------------
  # 2. Linear model coefficients
  #------------------------------------
  
  resultsLM = PEP_obs.df %>% 
    
    # group by moving windows
    group_by(MWyear) %>% 
    
    # get means for each moving window
    summarise(start  = mean(get(paste0(variable, "_start"))),
              length = mean(get(paste0(variable, "_length"))),
              end    = mean(leaf_out_mean)) %>% 
    
    do({
      
      # run models
      model1 = lm(start  ~ MWyear, data=.)
      model2 = lm(length ~ MWyear, data=.)
      model3 = lm(end    ~ MWyear, data=.)
      
      # merge data
      rbind(data.frame(tidy(model1), glance(model1)),
            data.frame(tidy(model2), glance(model2)),
            data.frame(tidy(model3), glance(model3)))})%>%            
    filter(!term %in% c("(Intercept)"))
  
  #------------------------------------
  # 3. Create plots
  #------------------------------------
  
  # Start date
  ############
  
  Start = ggplot(PEP_obs.df, aes(x = MWyear, y = as.Date(get(paste0(variable, "_start")), origin = "2000-01-01"))) + 
    # per-species mean lines 
    stat_summary(mapping = aes(group=species, color=species), fun = mean, geom = "line") +
    # overall mean line (thicker)
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth=1.5) +
    # linear trend over all series in each facet
    geom_smooth(method='lm', formula = y~x, se = FALSE, linetype="dashed", color="black") +
    # palette
    scale_color_manual(values = palette_values) +
    # limits
    coord_cartesian(xlim=c(1967.9,2007.1), ylim=c(as.Date('2000-01-25'),as.Date('2000-04-01')))+
    # MW window tick labels
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1966,2008,by=5),
                       labels = c('','','','','','','','',''))+
    # text annotation 
    annotate(geom="text", x=Inf, y = as.Date(Inf, origin="1970-01-01"), vjust=1.5, hjust=1.05, 
             label=paste0(round(resultsLM[1,]$estimate*10,1), 
                          ' ± ', 
                          round(resultsLM[1,]$std.error*20,1),
                          ' days per decade, R2 = ', 
                          round(resultsLM[1,]$r.squared,2)))+
    
    xlab("") + ylab('Date')+
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust=1))+
    plotTheme1
  
  # Preaseason length
  ###################
  
  Length = ggplot(PEP_obs.df, aes(x = MWyear, y = get(paste0(variable, "_length")))) + 
    # per-species mean lines 
    stat_summary(mapping = aes(group=species, color=species), fun = mean, geom = "line") +
    # overall mean line (thicker)
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth=1.5) +
    # linear trend over all series in each facet
    geom_smooth(method='lm', formula = y~x, se = FALSE, linetype="dashed", color="black") +
    # palette
    scale_color_manual(values = palette_values) +
    # limits
    coord_cartesian(xlim=c(1967.9,2007.1), ylim=c(35,80))+
    # MW window tick labels
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1966,2008,by=5),
                       labels = c('','','','','','','','',''))+
    # text annotation 
    annotate(geom="text", x=Inf, y = as.Date(Inf, origin="1970-01-01"), vjust=1.5, hjust=1.05, 
             label=paste0(round(resultsLM[2,]$estimate*10,1), 
                          ' ± ', 
                          round(resultsLM[2,]$std.error*20,1),
                          ' days per decade, R2 = ', 
                          round(resultsLM[2,]$r.squared,2)))+
    
    xlab("") + ylab('Days')+
    plotTheme1+
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust=1), 
          legend.position   = "none")
  
  # End date
  ##########
  
  End = ggplot(PEP_obs.df, aes(x = MWyear, y = as.Date(leaf_out_mean, origin = "2000-01-01"))) + 
    # per-species mean lines 
    stat_summary(mapping = aes(group=species, color=species), fun = mean, geom = "line") +
    # overall mean line (thicker)
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth=1.5) +
    # linear trend over all series in each facet
    geom_smooth(method='lm', formula = y~x, se = FALSE, linetype="dashed", color="black") +
    # palette
    scale_color_manual(values = palette_values) +
    # limits
    coord_cartesian(xlim=c(1967.9,2007.1), ylim=c(as.Date('2000-03-19'),as.Date('2000-05-24')))+
    # MW window tick labels
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1966,2008, by=5),
                       labels = c('1966-1980','1971-1985','1976-1990',
                                  '1981-1995','1986-2000','1991-2005',
                                  '1996-2010','2001-2015','2006-2020'))+
    # text annotation 
    annotate(geom="text", x=Inf, y = as.Date(Inf, origin="1970-01-01"), vjust=1.5, hjust=1.05, 
             label=paste0(round(resultsLM[3,]$estimate*10,1), 
                          ' ± ', 
                          round(resultsLM[3,]$std.error*20,1),
                          ' days per decade, R2 = ', 
                          round(resultsLM[3,]$r.squared,2)))+
    
    xlab("") + ylab('Date')+
    plotTheme1+
    theme(axis.text.x          = element_text(angle = 45, hjust=1),
          legend.position   = "none")
  
  #------------------------------------
  # 4. Merge and save plots
  #------------------------------------
  
  #define plot layout
  layout <- "
  A
  B
  C"
  
  #Merge plots
  Plot = Start + Length + End + 
    plot_layout(design = layout) + plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'a')&
    theme(plot.tag = element_text(face = 'bold'))
  
  print(Plot)
  
  #save plots as .pdf
  ggsave(Plot, file=paste0("Preseason_length_observations_", variable, ".pdf"), path=output.dir,
         width=6, height=10)
  
}


#==========================================
# Start dates of observations + simulations 
#==========================================

for (variable in variables) {

  #----------------------------------------------------------
  # 1) Summarize to (phenology, MWyear) time series
  #----------------------------------------------------------
  
  dat <- PEP.df %>%
    group_by(phenology, MWyear) %>%
    summarise(start  = mean(.data[[paste0(variable, "_start")]],  na.rm = TRUE),
              .groups = "drop")

  #----------------------------------------------------------
  # 2) Fit models by phenology (time series within each phenology)
  #----------------------------------------------------------
  
  resultsLM <- dat %>%
    group_by(phenology) %>%
    group_modify(~{
      m <- lm(start  ~ MWyear, data = .x)
      tidy(m) %>% 
        filter(term == "MWyear") %>%
        mutate(r.squared = glance(m)$r.squared) 
    }) %>%
    ungroup()

  #----------------------------------------------------------
  # 3) Build facet-aware labels
  #----------------------------------------------------------
  
  phenology_levels <- c("leaf_out","leaf_out_GDD","leaf_out_GDD.chill.lo","leaf_out_GDD.chill.hi","leaf_out_GDD.chill.DL")
  
  # Labels for the "start" panel text (per phenology)
 start_labs <- resultsLM %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    phenology = factor(phenology, levels = phenology_levels),
    # Label y-position (inside your coord_cartesian range)
    y_lab = as.Date("2000-03-27"),
    label = paste0(
      # slope ± 95% CI (days per decade)
      round(estimate * 10, 1), " \u00B1 ", 
      round(1.96 * std.error * 10, 1),
      " days per decade; R\u00B2 = ", 
      round(r.squared, 2)
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(phenology, y_lab, label)
  
  #----------------------------------------------------------
  # 4) Plot
  #----------------------------------------------------------

  Start.plot <-
    
    PEP.df %>%
    mutate(phenology = factor(phenology, levels = phenology_levels)) %>%
    
    ggplot(aes(x = MWyear,
               y = as.Date(.data[[paste0(variable, "_start")]], origin = "2000-01-01"))) +
    # per-species mean lines 
    stat_summary(aes(group = species, color = species), fun = mean, geom = "line") +
    # overall mean line (thicker)
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", size = 1.5) +
    # linear trend over all series in each facet
    geom_smooth(method = 'lm', formula = y ~ x, se = FALSE,
                linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    # palette
    scale_color_manual(values = palette_values) +
    # limits
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1967.9, 2007.1),
                    ylim = c(as.Date('2000-01-10'), as.Date('2000-03-25'))) +
    # MW window tick labels
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1966, 2008, by = 5),
                       labels = c('1966-1980','1971-1985','1976-1990',
                                  '1981-1995','1986-2000','1991-2005',
                                  '1996-2010','2001-2015','2006-2020')) +
    # facet-specific annotation (one row per phenology)
    geom_text(data = start_labs,
              aes(x = Inf, y = y_lab, label = label),
              inherit.aes = FALSE, hjust = 1.05, vjust = 1.2, size = 3.5) +

    xlab("") + ylab("Date") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
    facet_grid(~ phenology) +
    plotTheme1
  
  # Save plot
  ggsave(Start.plot, file = paste0("Preseason_start_", variable, ".pdf"),
         path = output.dir, width = 16, height = 4)

  print(Start.plot)
}
```

</details>

<br>

# 3. Reproducibility

<details>

<summary>show</summary>

```{r}
# Datetime
Sys.time()

# Session info
sessionInfo()
```

</details>
